name: Digital Evidence Collection Workflow
description: Preserves digital evidence with chain of custody documentation during incident response. A code node captures volatile evidence (running processes, network connections, memory state) from the affected endpoint. Riggs AI performs a malware assessment on the collected artifacts. An approval gate ensures the IR lead authorizes evidence packaging before it is formally preserved. A Jira ticket tracks the evidence chain of custody, and the forensics team is notified via Slack. All evidence handling steps are logged for legal admissibility.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Collect volatile evidence from affected endpoints including process lists and network connections

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create chain of custody tracking ticket for collected evidence

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify forensics team about evidence collection completion

config.description: Triggers when a high or critical severity incident requires digital evidence preservation

config.description: Collect volatile evidence from the endpoint before it is lost — processes, network connections, loaded modules

config.additional_instructions: Analyze the collected volatile evidence for indicators of compromise. Identify suspicious processes, unusual network connections, and potentially malicious loaded modules.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of collected volatile evidence to identify suspicious artifacts and IOCs
